vulnerability.title: Disable Kernel Parameter for Accepting ICMP Redirects by Default on IPv6 Interfaces
vulnerability.description: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then # Comment out any occurrences of net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects from /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf files for f in /etc/sysctl.d/*.conf /run/sysctl.d/*.conf /usr/local/lib/sysctl.d/*.conf; do # skip systemd-sysctl symlink (/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf -> /etc/sysctl.conf) if [[ "$(readlink -f "$f")" == "/etc/sysctl.conf" ]]; then continue; fi matching_list=$(grep -P '^(?!#).*[\s]*net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects.*$' $f | uniq ) if ! test -z "$matching_list"; then while IFS= read -r entry; do escaped_entry=$(sed -e 's|/|\\/|g' <<< "$entry") # comment out "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects" matches to preserve user data sed -i --follow-symlinks "s/^${escaped_entry}$/# &/g" $f done <<< "$matchin

triage_decision.reason: To set the runtime status of the net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects kernel parameter, run the following command: $ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0 To make sure that the setting is persistent, add the following line to a file in the directory /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; IPv6 networking; Kernel parameter modification

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for sysctl_net_ipv6_conf_default_accept_redirects (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sysctl_net_ipv6_conf_default_accept_redirects). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce JSON with steps: list commands, each with command and explanation.

We must fix: "Disable Kernel Parameter for Accepting ICMP Redirects by Default on IPv6 Interfaces". Likely kernel parameter net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects. We need to set it to 0. To make persistent, add line to /etc/sysctl.d: net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0.

Thus steps: 1) Use sysctl to set runtime: sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0. 2) Append line to /etc/sysctl.d file.

We need to use run_cmd for the shell command, read_file to inspect config before modifying, write_file to write/append.

Thus:

- Step 1: run_cmd: command: "sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0". Explanation: "Set the kernel parameter net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects to 0 at runtime."

- Step 2: read_file: path: "/etc/sysctl.d". Explanation: "Check if the directory exists and inspect its content." Actually read_file reads a single file; maybe we read the file to see if it exists; but we need to ensure we can write to it. Could read a placeholder like "/etc/sysctl.d/net_ipv6.conf_default_accept_redirects". But the instruction: "Use read_file to inspect the target config file BEFORE modifying it." So we read the file we intend to modify: maybe the file we will write to is "/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects". So read_file path: "/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects". Explanation: "Inspect the target config file before modifying."

- Step 3: write_file: path: "/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects". content: "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0". mode: "append". Explanation: "Append the setting to make it persistent."

We need JSON array of steps.

Thus output:

[
  {
    "command": "sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects=0",
    "explanation": "Set the kernel parameter net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects to 0 at runtime."
  },
  {
    "command": "read_file",
    "path": "/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects",
    "explanation": "Inspect the target config file before modifying."
  },
  {
    "command": "write_file",
    "path": "/etc/sysctl.d/net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects",
    "content": "net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = 0",
    "mode": "append",
    "explanation": "Append the setting to make it persistent."
  }
]

Check if any extra escaping needed. JSON should be valid.

Return.
